title: GitHub acquisition for 7.5 billion dollars in stock
decisionQuestion: Should Microsoft acquire GitHub for $7.5 billion in stock using existing share repurchase authorization?
situation: Microsoft is acquiring GitHub for $7.5 billion in stock, funded by a portion of its remaining ~$30 billion share repurchase authorization.
objectives: Acquire GitHub's developer platform and community
conditions: Acquisition price is $7.5 billion in Microsoft stock
constraints: Funding comes from the ~$30 billion share repurchase authorization

options.description: Proceed with the $7.5 billion stock acquisition
options.expectedBenefit: Owns the world's largest code hosting platform
options.principalRisk: Dilution from issuing stock and integration challenges

options.expectedBenefit: Preserves share repurchase authorization and avoids dilution

rejectedBenefit: Preserves share repurchase authorization and avoids dilution
observations: Acquisition price is $7.5 billion in stock
Remaining share repurchase authorization is ~$30 billion
inferences: Stock-for-stock deal links GitHub's value to Microsoft's share price

evidenceSpans.exactQuote: Microsoft will acquire GitHub for $7.5 billion in Microsoft stock.
Microsoft will use a portion of the remaining ~$30 billion of its current share repurchase authorization for the purchase.
